subject: [PATCH v2 3/4] mm: page_alloc: move capture_control to the page allocator

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the WRITE/READ once is now detached from the main barrier() and suggested adding a comment to explain its purpose.
reviews.raw_body:
Might be worth a comment to explain what the WRITE/READ once is dealing
with here since it's now detached from the main barrier(), but otherwise

Reviewed-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

2026-07-22.patch_summary: This patch moves the capture control from compaction's live compact control to the page allocator, allowing it to maintain its own copies of relevant information such as zone, migratetype, and allocation order. This change is necessary because defrag_mode promotes sub-block allocations to pageblock-order compaction, which can cause the allocation request order and compaction target order to differ. The patch also updates various functions in mm/compaction.c to pass the capture control through instead of a bare struct page pointer. No functional changes are introduced by this patch.
